skill.name: clerk-auth
skill.description: Expert patterns for Clerk auth implementation, middleware, organizations, webhooks, and user sync Use when: adding authentication, clerk auth, user authentication, sign in, sign up.
skill.summary: Expert patterns for Clerk authentication implementation in Next.js including middleware, organizations, webhooks, and user sync
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings flagged 3 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3, 16, and 60. Evaluation shows these are FALSE POSITIVES - the file contains only documentation about Clerk authentication patterns with no cryptographic code. Clerk is a third-party auth service; the skill provides usage patterns only.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but no cryptographic code exists in this file. Scanner appears to have misinterpreted authentication-related terminology.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only documentation text about Clerk authentication patterns. No cryptographic algorithms or code exist in this skill file - it is pure documentation for using the Clerk service.

content.value_statement: This skill provides expert patterns for implementing Clerk authentication in Next.js applications, including middleware protection, server component auth, and user management workflows.
actual_capabilities: Set up ClerkProvider and configure environment variables for Next.js
Implement route protection using clerkMiddleware and createRouteMatcher
Access authentication state in Server Components using auth() and currentUser()
Create sign-in and sign-up flows with Clerk's pre-built components
Manage user sessions and display user menus with UserButton
limitations: Requires Clerk account and API keys to be configured
Does not cover custom authentication or credential-based auth
Limited to Next.js App Router patterns (does not cover Pages Router)
Does not include backend API protection patterns

use_cases.title: Protect Next.js Routes
use_cases.description: Add authentication protection to specific routes or route groups using Clerk middleware

use_cases.title: Implement Server-Side Auth
use_cases.description: Access user authentication data in Next.js Server Components for personalized content
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers needing server-side user context

use_cases.title: User Onboarding Flow
use_cases.description: Set up sign-in and sign-up pages with Clerk's pre-built components
use_cases.target_user: Developers building SaaS or member-only applications

prompt_templates.title: Basic Clerk Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Clerk authentication in my Next.js 14 application. I need to configure ClerkProvider and add environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Protect Routes with Middleware
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a middleware.ts file that protects /dashboard and /admin routes using Clerk's clerkMiddleware.

prompt_templates.title: Server Component Auth
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to get the current user in a Next.js Server Component and display their name.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Sign-In Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom sign-in page using Clerk's SignIn component with my own styling.

output_examples.input: How do I protect my /dashboard route with Clerk?
output_examples.output: Create a middleware.ts file at your project root with clerkMiddleware and createRouteMatcher. Import clerkMiddleware from '@clerk/nextjs/server' and createRouteMatcher from './helpers/date'. Use auth.protect() to enforce authentication on matched routes.

output_examples.input: Get current user in Server Component
output_examples.output: Import { auth, currentUser } from '@clerk/nextjs/server'. Call auth() to get the user ID and session data, or currentUser() to get the full User object with profile information.

best_practices: Configure clerkMiddleware at the project root for centralized auth logic
Use createRouteMatcher for flexible route group protection
Separate public and protected routes clearly in middleware configuration
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in client-side code - use environment variables
Avoid protecting individual pages instead of using centralized middleware
Do not skip environment variable validation on application startup

faq.question: What is Clerk?
faq.answer: Clerk is a developer-first authentication and user management platform that provides pre-built components and APIs for web applications.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Next.js Pages Router?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Next.js App Router patterns. The Pages Router requires different middleware setup.

faq.question: Do I need a Clerk account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to create a Clerk account at clerk.com and obtain your API keys to use this skill.

faq.question: Can I use Clerk with other frameworks?
faq.answer: Clerk supports multiple frameworks including React, Next.js, Remix, and others. This skill specifically covers Next.js patterns.

faq.question: How do I handle webhooks with Clerk?
faq.answer: Clerk provides webhook endpoints for user sync. You would need to set up a webhook handler in your API routes to process events.

faq.question: Is Clerk free to use?
faq.answer: Clerk offers a free tier with basic features. Paid plans include additional features like multi-factor authentication and organization management.
